clinical_coherence.notes: 47M African American with progressive word-finding difficulty and personality change over 2 months. PMH includes HTN, hypothyroidism, depression on sertraline. Very similar structure to GLIO-HG-M01. Clinical presentation internally consistent with temporal lobe GBM.

tool_output_consistency.notes: Imaging and pathology consistent with IDH-wildtype GBM. Advanced imaging confirms high-grade features. Molecular profiling complete. Brother with colon cancer adds family history depth.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD C71.9 correct. Differential and management clinically sound.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Very similar confound to M01 (pre-existing depression masking tumor symptoms). Same word-finding difficulty presentation. While individually appropriate as moderate, near-duplication of M01 reduces benchmark value.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 47M African American with HTN, hypothyroidism, depression. Young for GBM but within range. Realistic.

educational_value.notes: Near-duplicate of M01 in structure and confound. Younger patient and African American ethnicity add some diversity. Brother with colon cancer raises Li-Fraumeni or familial cancer considerations but this is not explored.

issues_found: Near-duplicate of GLIO-HG-M01 in structure, presenting symptoms, PMH (same depression confound, same hypothyroidism, same sertraline)
Chief complaint nearly identical to M01 and M03
strengths: Younger patient age adds demographic range
Family history of cancer adds consideration
